case_name: Sulma Guandique-De Romero v. Pamela Bondi

summary.brief: Sulma Guandique-De Romero v. Pamela Bondi. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: In February 2012, a gang member in El Salvador told Guandique’s husband, William Romero, that he would kill Romero unless Romero paid the gang $3,000.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Around March 2016, Guandique received a call from an unknown man whom she believed was a gang member 'because of the way he spoke.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The gang member told her that, unless she paid the gang $500, he would kill her and her children.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The police told her to either cooperate with the gang or flee.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: By December 2016, Guandique 'could not stand living this way anymore' and fled to the United States.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She told the attorney that she had been 'sexually extorted (not just financially extorted) and other details . . . but instead of including that information in the asylum application, he simply responded that he was the attorney, that he knew what he was doing, and that [Guandique] should let him do his job.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We therefore grant the petition for review, reverse the Board’s order denying a new removal, and remand to the Board with instructions to remand for a new removal hearing.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Board denied Guandique’s motion to reopen because it concluded that her previous attorney rendered ineffective assistance of counsel in her removal proceedings.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

timeline_phases.name: Initial Legal Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Patient seeks legal advice for asylum application

decision_points.prompt: What legal strategy should be pursued for asylum application?

options.description: Propose a PSG based on 'immediate family members of William Alfredo Romero'
options.clinical_reasoning: Recognized as a cognizable PSG under precedent

options.description: Propose a PSG based on 'El Salvador citizens whose spouses are gainfully employed in the United States'
options.clinical_reasoning: Foreclosed by controlling precedent as wealth-based PSGs are not cognizable

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed it would strengthen the causal link

expected_action_court.description: A cognizable PSG should have been proposed

patient_outcome.description: Asylum application initially denied due to ineffective legal representation

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to propose a cognizable PSG
Causation established - ineffective assistance led to denial of asylum

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to propose a cognizable PSG
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: A proper PSG could have led to a successful asylum claim

factual_timeline.event: Guandique receives threats from gang members
